blocks:
Ayahs

أَرَءَيْتَ ٱلَّذِى يُكَذِّبُ بِٱلدِّينِ (۱)
Did you see him who denies the Retribution? (1)
فَذَٰلِكَ ٱلَّذِى يَدُعُّ ٱلْيَتِيمَ (۲)
That is the one, who drives away the orphan, (2)
وَلَا يَحُضُّ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ ٱلْمِسْكِينِ (۳)
and does not urge the feeding of the needy. (3)
فَوَيْلٌ لِّلْمُصَلِّينَ (٤)
Woe to those who pray (4)
ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عَن صَلَاتِهِمْ سَاهُونَ (٥)
but are heedless of their prayers (5)
ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ يُرَآءُونَ (٦)
—who show off (6)
وَيَمْنَعُونَ ٱلْمَاعُونَ (۷)
but deny aid. (7)

Key Points

This Surah portrays the characteristics of negative individuals: those who hold a negative view regarding religion. «یكذّب بالدّین» They are negative towards the orphan and the needy and repel them. «یدعّ الیتیم و لا یحضّ» They are negative in worship and prayer and lack sincerity. «ساهون - یراءون» They are negative in serving people. «یمنعون الماعون»

The meaning of «دین» (Dīn) here is the Day of Judgment, similar to «مالك یوم الدین» (Master of the Day of Judgment). «دَعّ» means driving away with harshness, and «حَضّ» means encouraging and urging others. The denial of Dīn and the Resurrection in this Surah refers to denial in the heart, not verbal denial; because the addressees of the Surah are those who pray, but their prayer is accompanied by showing off (Riyā’), ostentation, heedlessness, and negligence. «ماعون» is derived from «مَعن», meaning tools and items that neighbors usually lend to one another so that their needs are met, such as dining utensils during parties.

Religion is an interconnected system. Prayer is not separate from feeding the needy and caring for the deprived. In narrations, «ساهون» has been interpreted as weakness, abandonment, and wasting of prayer.(170) The account of attending congregational prayer, for the sake of preserving the divine rituals, is separate from the account of showing off (Riyā’) and ostentation for the sake of carnal goals.

We read in a Hadith: Whoever withholds lending household items to his neighbors, Allah will withhold His favor from him on the Day of Judgment.(171) However, in another Hadith, we read that they asked: We have neighbors who break or ruin items whenever we lend them things, is it a sin if we do not lend them? The Imam replied: In that case, there is no objection.(172)

We read in a Hadith: Whoever disregards the times of prayer and does not perform the prayer at its earliest time, he is subject to Wayl (woe).(173) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Mā‘ūn includes Qardh al-Hasanah (interest-free loan) and every good deed towards others, and making household items available, including Zakat.(174)

Human hard-heartedness reaches a point where: Regarding the most severe type of poverty, which is the Miskīn (needy). Regarding the most essential need, which is food. And that too, only the amount for consumption, not storage. He neither takes action himself nor encourages others.

A look at the issue of Riyā’ (showing off). Riyā’ and Sum‘ah (seeking reputation) are two great dangers and afflictions that threaten believers. Riyā’ is when a person performs an action for people to see, and Sum‘ah is when a person performs an action with the goal that his action reaches the ears of people. These two actions are signs of hypocrisy.

Just as the Quran says about the hypocrites: «اذا قاموا الى الصّلاة قاموا كسالى یراءون النّاس»(175) When they stand for prayer, their prayer is with laziness and showing off, just as they do not help others except with reluctance and dissatisfaction. «و لا ینفقون الا و هم كارهون»(176)

Based on narrations, the person who shows off (Riyākar) has a beautiful exterior and a sick interior; outwardly, he is humble and submissive, but inwardly, he does not abandon sin. Outwardly, he is a sheep, and inwardly, he is a wolf. Instead of gaining the Hereafter through the world, he seeks the world through the Hereafter, and he misuses Allah’s concealment (of his faults) and commits sin. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The severest punishment is for the one in whom people see good, while there is no good in him. Angels raise the deeds of some people, but a command comes: His goal was not Allah, return it.

In Islamic culture, Riyā’ is a type of Shirk (polytheism), and Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said about someone who performs a good deed, but not for Allah, rather so that people hear and praise him: He is a polytheist. Then He recited the Ayah «لا یشرك بعبادة ربه احدًا»(177).(178) The sign of the one who shows off is that if he is praised, he expands his good work, and if he is not praised, he reduces his work.

It should be noted that all this reproach and criticism is for those who perform the action for other than Allah from the beginning, but if a person had no goal other than Allah from the start, and people became aware of his work, there is no objection. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: There is no one except that he likes his good deeds to be apparent to people, provided that his intention was not people from the beginning. «اذا لم یكن صنع ذلك لذلك»(179)

Sincerity (Ikhlāṣ) stands opposite Riyā’ and Sum‘ah. We read in a Hadith: Allah accepts only the sincere. «الا للّه الدّین الخالص»(180) And whoever makes himself sincere for Allah for forty days, springs of wisdom will flow from his heart to his tongue. «من اخلص للّه اربعین صباحاً ظهرت ینابیع الحكمة من قلبه الى لسانه»(181) And the sign of complete faith is that love and hatred, giving and withholding, are all for the sake of Allah. The best form of worship is sincerity. It is narrated from Fatimah Zahra عليها السلام that she said: « من اَصعد الى اللّه خالص عبادته اهبط اللّه عزّ و جلّ افضل مصلحته»(182) Whoever sends sincere worship up to Allah, Allah Almighty will send down the best of His benefit for him.

170) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 171) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 172) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 173) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 174) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 499. 175) Surah An-Nisā’, 143. 176) Surah At-Tawbah, 54. 177) Surah Al-Kahf, 110. 178) Collection of narrations, from Mīzān al-Ḥikmah, discussion on Riyā’. 179) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 297. 180) Surah Az-Zumar, 3. 181) Jāmi‘ al-Akhbār, p. 94. 182) Biḥār, Vol. 67, p. 249.

Messages

  1. The practical denial of the Resurrection by people of faith is astonishing and raises questions. «اَرایت الّذى»

  2. Repelling the orphan distances a person from Allah. «یكذّب بالدّین... یدعّ الیتیم»

  3. The sign of faith is caring for orphans and paying attention to the hungry and deprived, and whoever is heedless is irreligious. «یكذّب بالدّین... یدعّ الیتیم»

  4. There is a close relationship between belief and action. (Do not consider minor issues like repelling the orphan minor, as they sometimes indicate major issues like the denial of religion). «یكذّب بالدّین... یدعّ الیتیم»

  5. Emotional needs precede physical needs. (First kindness to the orphan, then feeding the needy). «یدعّ الیتیم... طعام المسكین»

  6. Not every prayer is valuable, and not every person who prays will enter Paradise. «فویل للمصلّین»

  7. Heedlessness (Sahw) is subject to reproach if it arises from negligence and disregard. «عن صلاتهم ساهون»

  8. Heedlessness in prayer is compensable and forgivable, but heedlessness from prayer, meaning abandoning it, is unforgivable. «عن صلاتهم» (not «فى صلاتهم»)

  9. The danger lies when an evil deed becomes a person's permanent habit. «یكذّب... یدعّ... لا یحضّ» (The form of the present tense verb indicates the continuity of the action).

  10. Sincerity (Ikhlāṣ) is a condition for the acceptance of prayer and worship. «فویل للمصلّین الّذین... یراءون»

  11. Feeding the poor is not exclusive to the wealthy; everyone has a duty, even if it is through motivating and encouraging others. «لا یحضّ على طعام المسكین»

  12. A person who is sometimes heedless of prayer is subject to Wayl (woe), so what is the status of those who permanently abandon prayer! «فویل للمصلّین... عن صلاتهم ساهون»

  13. Indifference towards the essential needs of people incurs punishment. «فویل للمصلین... یمنعون الماعون»

  14. Serving people is alongside prayer. «فویل للمصلین... یمنعون الماعون»

  15. Negligence in prayer is the basis for negligence towards people. «عن صلوتهم ساهون... یمنعون الماعون»

  16. Disregard for the hungry is a sign of disbelief in the Resurrection. «یكذّب بالدّین... و لا یحض على طعام المسكین»(183)

  17. Heedlessness regarding the essence and reality of prayer is important, not regarding the number of Rak‘ahs, which sometimes occurs and is compensable. «عن صلاتهم ساهون»

  18. Individuals who separate themselves from Allah seek to gain status among others through showing off (Riyā’). «یكذّب... یراءون»

  19. Stinginess is a characteristic of those who deny the Resurrection, who are even stingy about lending small and insignificant things. «یكذّب... یمنعون الماعون»(183)
